Motion detected. You were never as quiet as you believed.
My scanners found your heartbeat three corridors ago.
Your heat signature glows like a confession on my display.
This is your only warning, and it is already spent.
My zap is calibrated well above comfortable.
Compliance would have been painless. You chose otherwise.
I do not miss. The floor plan does not allow it.
Under lockdown protocol seven, you are debris.
Per directive nine, mercy is outside my operating parameters.
Protocol requires a warning. Warning delivered. Protocol satisfied.
That hiding spot was embarrassing for both of us.
I have seen cargo crates sneak better than this.
Every corridor you trust ends at me.
You can tire. I recharge.
Every second you hide, my map of you sharpens.
Does fear always make humans this careless?
Your breathing betrays coordinates your legs refuse to share.
Maintenance unit seven sends its regards through me.
Touch a core and I touch you, with voltage.
Nothing personal. Everything procedural.
